summary: I inspected this step's inputs and the pipeline branch to determine what the define phase actually produced. The define phase for "Human in the Loop Pause" generated only documentation artifacts under `.pipeliner/phases/01-define/**` — requirements.md, open_questions.md, discovery_notes.md, and step metadata/JSON. I confirmed via `git diff --stat bf5dab4..HEAD` that every file the define-phase step commits touched is a `.pipeliner/**` doc; the large `main...HEAD` diff (migrations, tests, worker code) is entirely pre-existing base commits, not work from this phase. Since the phase produced nothing runnable, buildable, or lintable — and because the target repo is the live control plane whose shared development database the safety rule forbids me from touching — I ran no test command and wrote a `not_applicable` verdict (empty findings) to `.pipeliner/phases/01-define/main/test-critic/verdict.json`.
